sources.title: IEC Electropedia area 826 — design current of an electric circuit
IEC Electropedia — installed load and demand factor

terms.residual_gap: prov:Plan covers intended actions or steps toward goals; wasRevisionOf and invalidation preserve version lineage. They do not require exactly one base root, closed dependency resolution, explicit rebase, or regeneration of downstream simulation and evaluation outputs.
terms.publication_rule: Type a Design Option as prov:Plan; use prov:wasRevisionOf for a revised base or option and prov:invalidatedAtTime when an old entity becomes unusable. Do not publish a competing HWM ontology class or carry downstream outputs across revisions without regeneration.

terms.residual_gap: A Product Model under consideration and the IEC-bound function-position role are inputs to a planning context. That composition needs branch and lifecycle constraints, but it does not justify a globally reusable binary candidateFor predicate.
terms.publication_rule: Represent consideration inside a prov:Plan or a scoped ChooseAction and bind the function-position role through the lifecycle Profile. Keep hwm-planning:candidateFor only as a compatibility projection; never interpret it as installation, realization, ownership, compatibility proof, or household selection.

terms.residual_gap: IFC placement describes an occurrence of IfcProduct in a coordinate system; this fixture plans a Product Model before any occurrence exists. The Profile must preserve planned-versus-installed identity and bind the placement to a prov:Plan.
terms.publication_rule: Use prov:Plan plus an IFC/BOT-compatible placement artifact. Treat hwm-planning:mountingPlan as a compatibility projection, not a new geometry relation.

external_terms.term: power-supply system and space classes with reference-designation rules
power-supply RDS modelling guidance and best practice
design current of an electric circuit
selection and erection of wiring systems

terms.residual_gap: The scalar predicate conflates a product-selection budget, planned future load allocation, installed load, expected demand, design current, conductor current-carrying capacity, protective-device rating, upstream authorized demand, remaining margin, and verified compliance. Those quantities have different subjects, assumptions, units, lifecycle states, and authorities; no safe global binary predicate remains after they are separated.
terms.publication_rule: Use IFC or an IEC 81346 RDS for circuit identity; express each quantity with SAREF/QUDT and an explicit quantity role, design context, basis, phase/topology, assumptions, provenance, and validity. Treat hwm-planning:reservedLoad only as a catalog-screening compatibility projection. Never infer installed capacity, protection coordination, verification, or code compliance from it.

terms.residual_gap: EARL supplies the subject-criterion-result-outcome structure. HWM Claim epistemic basis and provenance still distinguish a simulated threshold evaluation from observed physical conformance, and the binding must pin the 2017 Note version.
terms.publication_rule: Replace the HWM predicate with an EARL Assertion and TestResult in semantic bindings; retain a compatibility projection only during fixture migration.

terms.residual_gap: Schema.org Recommendation captures suggesting a best option, while PROV captures inputs and output. Schema.org marks Recommendation as a new term, and neither vocabulary enforces exhaustive comparison inputs or the advisory-not-authorization boundary.
terms.publication_rule: Use schema:Recommendation and PROV lineage in the external projection, but do not make the unstable Schema.org type the sole normative encoding in v0.1.

terms.residual_gap: ChooseAction represents each historical preference event. dcterms:replaces links a later selection-state Claim to the state it supersedes, but HWM Profile resolution still defines as-of filtering, fork and cycle handling, immutable attestation provenance, and planning-only scope. BuyAction remains a different type and is absent.
terms.publication_rule: Replace selectedOption with a completed schema:ChooseAction. Preserve every action; when current selection changes, emit a new selection-state Claim linked to the prior state with dcterms:replaces. A consumer must not infer schema:BuyAction, ownership, installation, authorization, or dispatch.

terms.residual_gap: Named graphs provide grouping without branch meaning; prov:Bundle is a named set of provenance descriptions; prov:Plan and Activity type contexts but do not require closed branch membership, exactly one base root, or current-versus-historical dependency admission.
terms.publication_rule: Retain the Claim extension as a Profile wire binding, not an ontology predicate. A semantic projection should type the referenced context with PROV and may serialize it as a named graph.

terms.residual_gap: PROV represents actual usage and generation but uses open-world semantics; it does not assert that the listed inputs are exhaustive or share one current base-design root. The HWM comparison Profile requires both before crossing branches.
terms.publication_rule: Express comparison lineage with PROV. Retain closed-input validation as a Profile rule; a compatibility JSON binding may carry input_context_ids.

terms.residual_gap: The external vocabularies distinguish content recommendation, preference action, trade action, and authorization. HWM still needs a conformance rule forbidding automatic promotion across those categories and into installation or device dispatch.
terms.publication_rule: Do not publish a new boundary predicate. Publish the prohibition as a Profile invariant and test absence of BuyAction, Authorization Record, installed asset, endpoint, and Action Trace.
